scene: I came while it was still dark
title: Apostle to the Apostles
narration: We three are walking back from the tomb, the other Marys and I.
We have not reached the city, nor the apostles, yet.
We hurry. We are running, almost.
I had to come while it was still dark. I had to be first.
No one watched that sepulchre more closely than I did.
And now, suddenly, on the road, He meets us.
He comes toward us. He, risen. He, alive.
He speaks softly, gently, lifting us up from our grief.
He says one word: Avete. Rejoice. Hail.
And I think, even as He says it, of Eve.
The first woman heard a curse. We hear a greeting.
Out of Eva, He forms this Ave. The old word is undone.
I am glad beyond what any tongue could say.
I run to Him. I believe now. The faith burns in me like light.
And I do what was kept from me before, when I sought the living among the dead.
I take hold of His feet. I kiss them.
I feel the flesh in which He rose. Real. Warm. His.
By this touch I am made certain of the rising.
We fall down. We adore Him as God and Lord.
Then He sees the fear still clinging to us, and He says: Do not be afraid.
He drives the fear out as He had driven out the sorrow.
And He calls us to His own: Go, tell my brethren.
His brethren. He who is Lord, He names them brothers.
Tell them to go into Galilee. There they will see Him.
He sends me, a woman, with the good news on this morning.
I who was the door of death now first proclaim the rising.
I run to the mourning disciples to say: I have seen the Lord.
I have seen Him, and He told me these things to tell to you.
prayer: O Jesus, my redemption, my love and my desire,
You who, rising from the dead, willed to meet the women returning from Your tomb,
I too seek You, the one my soul loves, sweetest Jesus.
I seek, and do not yet find: come, then, to my soul that seeks You.
If You withhold Your desirable presence now,
do not take it from me forever;
but in the last resurrection of the faithful
show Yourself to me, gentle and kind, with Your chosen ones,
that I may rejoice in Your presence everlastingly. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Quia vidi Dominum, et hæc, quæ dicta sunt, dixit mihi.
latinJewel.english: For I have seen the Lord, and these things that were spoken, He said to me.
